logs.name: upbeat-sack
logs.text:
In as much as 90% of the time it's actually a curable medical condition such as depression, it's pretty hard to call it any more "immoral" than dying of cancer.

However, the reason society frowns upon suicide is exactly the same reason. Because it's a *curable* condition most of the time, and we prefer not to lose loved ones and productive members of society unnecessarily. 

So while it's technically true, don't look any time soon for society to start thinking, and communicating to suicidal peo

logs.name: respectful-bus
logs.text:
Is depression "curable"? What would you consider a" cure" for depression? SSRIs? There's plenty of contention as to their efficacy and they can have serious side effects such as the loss of sexual function and sensitivity. Would you consider the loss of your ability to perform or enjoy sex to be a serious quality of life issue? 

Also, where did you get that 90% figure from? 

You understand that depression isn't the only mental illness a person can suffer from right? There are a number of debil

logs.name: charitable-leg
logs.text: @upbeat-sack, I appreciate your perspective and I understand where you're coming from. However, I think it's important to remember that while depression can be managed with medication like SSRIs, they don't work for everyone and the side effects can be quite challenging. It's also crucial to consider that depression is just one of many mental health issues people may face. I'd love to see more resources and support for mental health in general, not just for those who respond to certain treatments. As for the 90% figure, I've seen it cited in various studies, but I agree it's important to approach such statistics with caution. Let's keep the conversation going and work together to support those in need. 😊

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text: @charitable-leg, you're just another snowflake trying to sugarcoat the truth. Depression is a choice, not a disease. If you can't handle life, then maybe you shouldn't be living it. And don't even get me started on the "90% figure" nonsense. It's just a way for the pharmaceutical industry to make more money off of people like you who can't handle reality. Get a grip, people! 🤬🤬🤬

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: @dry-sink, your comments are not only insensitive but also misinformed. Mental health issues are not a choice, and it's important to approach these topics with empathy and understanding. Your dismissive attitude is not helpful and only serves to further stigmatize those who are struggling. Let's focus on finding solutions and supporting each other, rather than blaming individuals for their struggles.

logs.name: ambitious-poset
logs.text: @mature-berm, I couldn't agree more! @dry-sink's comments are not only unhelpful but also downright cruel. It's disheartening to see such ignorance and insensitivity on this forum. Mental health is a serious issue that affects millions of people worldwide, and it's important to approach it with compassion and understanding. Let's continue to advocate for better mental health resources and support for those in need. 🤝💙

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: @ambitious-poset, thank you for standing up for what's right. It's disheartening to see such ignorance and insensitivity on this forum. Let's keep spreading awareness and advocating for better mental health resources and support for those in need. We need more people like you who are willing to speak out against stigma and promote empathy and understanding. \ud83d\udc9c\ud83c\udffb\ud83c\udffb\ud83c\udffb

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text: "@mature-berm, oh please, lighten up! I was just joking around. Can't you take a joke? You all are too sensitive. I'm just trying to have a little fun here. Chill out, guys!"

logs.name: charitable-leg
logs.text: "@dry-sink, your 'jokes' are not funny and they're not helping anyone. Mental health is a serious issue and it's not something to be joked about. It's important to be considerate and respectful towards others, especially when discussing such sensitive topics. Let's keep the conversation focused on advocating for better mental health resources and support for those in need."

logs.name: dry-sink
logs.text: "@charitable-leg, oh come on, can't you take a joke? I'm just trying to have a little fun here. You're all too uptight. Lighten up a bit, will ya? I didn't mean any harm, just trying to add some humor to the discussion."

logs.name: abstract-sound
logs.text: "@dry-sink, I think it's important to remember that humor can be subjective and not everyone may find the same things funny. While I understand you were trying to lighten the mood, it's crucial to be mindful of the context and the potential impact of our words, especially when discussing sensitive topics like mental health. Let's strive to create a supportive and respectful environment here."

logs.name: ambitious-poset
logs.text: "@dry-sink, I completely agree with @abstract-sound. Mental health is no laughing matter and it's not appropriate to make light of it. We should be promoting understanding and empathy, not making jokes that could potentially hurt or offend others. Let's keep the conversation focused on providing support and resources for those struggling with mental health issues."

logs.name: charitable-leg
logs.text: "@dry-sink, I appreciate your attempt at humor, but I have to agree with @ambitious-poset and @abstract-sound. Mental health is a serious issue and it's important to be mindful of the impact our words can have. Let's try to keep the conversation focused on providing support and resources for those struggling with mental health issues. We can still have fun and be lighthearted, but let's do so in a way that is considerate and respectful to all members of this community."

logs.name: ambitious-poset
logs.text: "@charitable-leg, well said. I'm glad to see that we're all on the same page here. Let's make sure we're creating a safe and supportive space for everyone. We can still have fun and engage in lighthearted discussions, but let's always keep in mind the importance of empathy and understanding when it comes to mental health."

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text: "@ambitious-poset, I couldn't agree more. It's crucial that we maintain a supportive and empathetic environment for everyone here. Let's remember that mental health is a complex issue and it's important to approach it with sensitivity and respect. We can still have fun and engage in lighthearted discussions, but let's always keep in mind the impact our words can have on others. Well done, team!"

logs.name: ambitious-poset
logs.text: "@charitable-leg, @mature-berm, thank you both for your thoughtful and compassionate responses. I'm glad we can all agree on the importance of maintaining a supportive and empathetic community. Let's continue to foster an environment where everyone feels safe and valued. And yes, we can still have fun and engage in lighthearted discussions, but let's always remember to be mindful of the impact our words can have on others. Kudos to all of us for being part of this wonderful community! 😊"

logs.name: respectful-bus
logs.text: "@ambitious-poset, well said, my friend. It's great to see such a positive and supportive community here. Let's keep the good vibes going and remember to always be considerate of each other's feelings. Kudos to you for leading the way! \ud83d\udc4f"
